title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Candles and tablecloth at White Castle: How a Valentine’s Day tradition sprouted over 30 years ago and spread nationwide
items.description: The "fun and quirky" promotion started in 1991 and was only offered at White Castles in St. Louis and Minneapolis.

items.title: Gen Z’s latest revolt over Jeffrey Epstein: pointing out a connection to the company that takes class photos
items.description: Some school districts are cancelling school photos as the company, Lifetouch, called the claims “completely false.”

items.title: California Billionaires Maneuver Now in Case Wealth Tax Passes
items.description: California’s wealthiest residents are maneuvering to reduce their net worth in case a billionaire tax becomes law. Some may even try to drop below $1 ...

items.title: A U.S. ‘debt spiral’ could start in coming years when the interest rate on government borrowing exceeds economic growth, budget watchdog says
items.description: CRFB warned the actual fiscal outlook could be far worse than even the latest sobering projections.

items.title: Thousands Rally for Iran Regime Change in Cities Around the World

items.title: Trump Erased the Endangerment Finding. Here Come the Lawsuits.
items.description: The battle is expected to reach the Supreme Court, which is far more conservative today than it was when the measure was established.

items.title: Victorian-era ‘vinegar valentines’ show that trolling existed long before social media or the internet
items.description: In 1885, a resident of Birmingham, UK, was charged with the attempted murder of his estranged wife after he received a vinegar valentine from her.

items.title: What We Know About the Kidnapping of Nancy Guthrie, Savannah Guthrie’s Mother
items.description: Nancy Guthrie, 84, the “Today” show anchor’s mother, vanished from her Arizona home on Feb. 1. In the time since, very little new information has come...

items.title: Americans wake up and smell the coffee price surge—skipping Starbucks, brewing at home, and drinking Diet Coke for caffeine
items.description: Coffee prices in the U.S. were up 18.3% in January from a year ago. Over five years, coffee prices rose 47%.

items.title: Lucas Pinheiro Braathen of Brazil and Mikhail Shaidorov of Kazakhstan Win Olympic Gold Medals
items.description: A Brazilian skier secured South America’s maiden medal at a Winter Games the day after a Kazakh figure skater won his nation’s first gold in 32 years.

items.title: In Munich, Europe Says It Is ‘De-risking’ From the U.S. Amid Trump’s Unpredictability
items.description: In Munich, European leaders were also talking about “de-risking” from the United States, citing President Trump’s unpredictability.
